projects.name: F1 Predictions
projects.summary: Calibrated race-outcome forecasts for every Formula 1 Grand Prix.
projects.description: The MotorsportVerse flagship. A three-pathway prediction engine — qualifying-pace regression, Plackett-Luce probability calibration, and a per-lap Monte Carlo race simulator — with continuous-learning infrastructure (model registry, drift detection, A/B promotion) and a live static-export dashboard. Reference implementation for all other projects.

projects.name: RaceIQ F2
projects.summary: Sprint, feature-race, and championship forecasts for FIA Formula 2.
projects.description: A purpose-built spec-series model: a leakage-safe driver-skill blend (Elo with rookie pooling + finishing history) routed through two race-type heads — a merit feature race and a reverse-grid sprint — with Monte Carlo win/podium probabilities, finishing-range intervals, a sprint+feature-aware championship projection, forward-eval, and drift/promotion monitoring. Full website with per-round sprint/feature detail, head-to-head matrices, and an accuracy dashboard. Runs on the real 2026 FIA Formula 2 season — official results are scraped into a committed, reviewable snapshot with model forecasts for upcoming rounds, refreshed on a schedule. ~75% reuses MotorsportVerse core.
datasets: fiaformula2.com (official results, scraped to a committed snapshot)

projects.name: Formula E Predictions
projects.summary: Race and championship forecasts for the ABB FIA Formula E World Championship.
projects.description: The first all-electric series on the MotorsportVerse core: a driver-forward skill blend (Elo seeded from 2019+ seasons + finishing history + optional gradient-boosted signal) with post-qualifying grid conditioning, Monte Carlo win/podium probabilities calibrated separately for street and permanent circuits, and a championship projection that accounts for pole and fastest-lap bonus points. Runs on the real 2025-26 season: official results flow from the live Formula E API into a committed, reviewable snapshot, with doubleheader weekends modeled as separate rounds. Backfilled and backtested across twelve seasons of series history. Experimental while forward accuracy accrues over real rounds.
datasets: Formula E API (official results, snapshotted to a committed file)

projects.name: IndyCar Predictions
projects.summary: Race and championship forecasts for the NTT IndyCar Series.
projects.description: American open-wheel racing on the MotorsportVerse core, built snapshot-first: fifteen seasons of hand-verified historical data are the committed source of truth, and live updates land only behind strict parse-validation gates. The model's signature is a dual rating system that scores oval and road/street ability separately - validated to materially beat a single rating in walk-forward testing - alongside attrition modeling tuned to oval risk and Chevrolet/Honda engine effects. Covers the full 2026 season including the Indianapolis 500's 33-car field. Experimental while forward accuracy accrues over real rounds.
datasets: indycar.com / Wikipedia race classifications (validated refresh path)

projects.name: NASCAR Predictions
projects.summary: Race, stage, and Chase championship forecasts for the NASCAR Cup Series.
projects.description: Stock-car racing on the MotorsportVerse core: a field-relative driver-skill blend with per-track-type ratings (superspeedway, intermediate, short track, road course), attrition modeled as a first-class component of every forecast, and manufacturer effects. Title odds run through a format-aware championship simulator that encodes the 2026 Chase and the 2017-2025 elimination playoffs for historical backtesting, with the playoff projection gated on a passing seven-season backtest. Runs on the real 2026 Cup season: official results flow from NASCAR's public feeds into a committed, reviewable snapshot with per-stage results captured. Experimental while forward accuracy accrues over real rounds.
datasets: NASCAR official feeds (results + stages, snapshotted to committed files)

projects.name: RaceIQ F3
projects.summary: Sprint, feature-race, and championship forecasts for FIA Formula 3.
projects.description: The third series on the MotorsportVerse core, built at RaceIQ F2 parity: a leakage-safe driver-skill blend (Elo with rookie pooling + finishing history + optional gradient-boosted signal) routed through two race-type heads — a merit feature race and a reverse-top-12 sprint — with Monte Carlo win/podium probabilities, finishing-range intervals, and a sprint+feature-aware championship projection over the 30-car grid. Runs on the real 2026 FIA Formula 3 season: official results are scraped into a committed, reviewable snapshot via the shared FIA feeder-series source, with model forecasts for upcoming rounds. Experimental while forward accuracy accrues over real rounds.
datasets: fiaformula3.com (official results, scraped to a committed snapshot)

projects.name: IMSA Predictions
projects.summary: Multi-class forecasts for the IMSA SportsCar Championship.
projects.description: Scaffolded on the shared MotorsportVerse core: a IMSA project tree with DataSource and Predictor seams ready to implement. Inherits calibration, championship simulation, standings, evaluation, drift monitoring, and leakage guards from motorsport-core the moment a data feed is wired.
